questions: Should the 'Switch to Final Video' action calculate the video clip duration from the final video's actual duration, or should it force-fit to the shot group's time span? If the final video is shorter/longer than the group span, which takes precedence?
Should the dismiss state persist across sessions (e.g., stored in localStorage or DB), or is session-only (React state) sufficient?

When switching to final video, should the replacement clip go on the same track as the shot group, or should it go on a specific track? (The shot group is always on a single row/track, so same track seems natural.)
Should the badge also appear in the shot group's right-click context menu (alongside 'Jump to Shot' and 'Generate Video'), or only as a visual badge on the overlay?

success_criteria.criterion: Sky-blue badge appears on the right side of shot group overlays that have a final video in shot_final_videos
Badge is not shown for shot groups without final videos
Clicking badge opens context menu with 'Switch to Final Video' and 'Dismiss' options
'Switch to Final Video' removes all image clips in the shot group and replaces with a single video clip spanning the same time range
Replacement video clip is properly registered as an asset in the registry
'Dismiss' hides the badge for that shot group for the remainder of the session
Badge is hidden during drag/resize operations (same as shot group overlays)
New hooks follow existing patterns (useShotFinalVideos, useStaleVariants, useAssetManagement)
Badge styling is consistent with existing badge patterns (stale variant amber badge) but uses a distinct color
Each new file stays under ~150 lines
Context menu reuses existing portal/menu patterns from TimelineCanvas
Manual smoke test: full flow works end-to-end in browser with real final video data

assumptions: Dismiss state is session-only (React useState) — no persistence to DB or localStorage. This keeps the implementation simple and matches the stale variant dismiss pattern.

The replacement video clip will span the exact time range of the shot group (minStart to maxEnd of constituent clips), regardless of the actual video duration. The clip's from/to will be set to 0 and the video's actual duration respectively, with speed adjusted if needed to fill the span.
The badge goes on the shot group overlay (not on individual clips), since the action operates on the entire group.
The final video's MIME type is 'video/mp4' — matching the pattern in useAssetManagement.ts.
useShotFinalVideos hook can be called from the video-editor tool since it's a cross-tool shared hook (currently in travel-between-images). No import restrictions prevent this.
The badge color will be sky-500 (blue) to distinguish from the amber stale variant badge — 'ready/available' vs 'outdated'.
